questions: Should media_urls support a single URL string as well as a list, or strictly require an array?
For the publication record, is message_id=0 acceptable for the database schema (e.g. foreign key constraints, unique indexes on message_id+platform+action), or should we use a synthetic ID?
Should route_key be required in direct-post mode (since there's no channel to resolve a route from), or should we fall back to the guild's default route?

success_criteria.criterion: share_to_social accepts media_urls + tweet_text and posts a tweet with the downloaded media without requiring a Discord message reference
Existing message-based share_to_social flow is completely unchanged — all current parameters and behaviors work identically
Direct-post mode supports scheduled_at, action (post/reply), target_post, and route_key parameters
Direct-post mode returns error if tweet_text is missing
Media files are cleaned up from temp directory after publish (both success and failure)
All existing tests pass without modification
New code path adds fewer than 80 lines to execute_share_to_social
Tool schema description clearly documents when to use media_urls vs message_link/message_id
Direct-post mode tested end-to-end with a real Supabase media URL posting to Twitter

assumptions: media_urls will be a JSON array of URL strings; we will not accept a single string (the LLM can wrap it in an array)

route_key will be recommended but not strictly required in direct-post mode — if omitted, route resolution will use guild_id + channel_id=0 which should fall through to the guild's default route if one exists
message_id=0 and channel_id=0 are safe sentinel values for the publication record (no FK constraints in the social_publications table that would reject them)
user_id=0 is acceptable for direct posts since there is no Discord user associated with external media
The existing _download_media_from_url on sharer is reliable for Supabase URLs and does not need modification
Direct-post mode does not need duplicate detection since there is no stable message_id to deduplicate against
